results.name: Random Signature Injection
results.description: Submit valid Paillier-encrypted votes signed with a random 64-byte string.
results.threat_model: Attacker intercepts the submission channel and injects fabricated results.
results.evidence: Ed25519 verify_signature() returned False; submission persisted as rejected.
results.recommendation: ⚠ Signature verification may not be enforced on this endpoint.

results.name: Cross-Station Key Confusion
results.description: Sign station A's payload with station B's private key.
results.threat_model: Compromised officer B tries to submit fraudulent results on behalf of station A.
results.evidence: Backend compares signature against the registered public key of the submitting station.
results.recommendation: 🔴 CRITICAL: Cross-station key acceptance is a vote fraud vector.

results.name: Post-Sign Ciphertext Tampering
results.description: Modify the encrypted ciphertext after signing the payload.
results.threat_model: Man-in-the-middle alters encrypted votes in transit without the signing key.
results.evidence: Canonical payload serialised before verification — byte-level integrity guaranteed.
results.recommendation: 🔴 CRITICAL: Unsigned vote modification not detected.

results.name: Empty Signature Bypass
results.description: Submit with an empty signature string.
results.threat_model: Attacker omits signature hoping the field is not validated.
results.evidence: verify_signature() decodes empty b64 → InvalidSignature → False.
results.recommendation: 🔴 Empty signature accepted — input validation gap.

results.name: Exact Replay Attack
results.details: First submission failed (HTTP 409) — cannot run replay test.

results.name: Re-signed Replay
results.details: Skipped — no unused station with known key available.

results.name: Ghost Station Injection
results.description: Submit a result for a non-existent polling station ID.
results.threat_model: Attacker fabricates a station to inject votes outside the registered hierarchy.
results.evidence: Station existence validated against database before processing signature.

results.name: Extra Candidate Code Injection
results.description: Include an extra 'GHOST_CAND' entry in the encrypted votes.
results.threat_model: Attacker invents a new candidate to corrupt homomorphic aggregate keys.
results.evidence: Homomorphic aggregation will detect candidate mismatch across submissions.
results.recommendation: ✔ Extra candidate blocked at submission layer.

results.name: Malformed Ciphertext Injection
results.description: Submit ciphertext as a non-numeric string ('INVALID_HEX_NOT_A_NUMBER').
results.threat_model: Attacker supplies garbage ciphertext to crash the aggregation pipeline.
results.evidence: Python int() conversion raises ValueError on non-numeric ciphertext.
results.recommendation: ⚠ Malformed ciphertext accepted — will crash aggregation on int() cast.

results.name: Negative Vote Encoding
results.description: Encrypt negative vote counts (-500 per candidate) and submit signed.
results.threat_model: Attacker submits negative-encrypted votes to reduce opponent totals via homomorphic addition.
results.details: HTTP 409 | The backend cannot inspect ciphertext plaintext.
results.evidence: Paillier supports negative numbers — this is an inherent HE limitation.

results.name: Homomorphic Arithmetic Verification
results.details: Skipped — no unused station with known key available.

results.name: Audit Hash Chain Integrity
results.description: Verify SHA-256 hash chain covers all audit log entries without breaks.
results.threat_model: Database-level attacker silently edits or deletes audit log entries.
results.recommendation: ✔ Audit chain is intact — retroactive tampering detectable.

results.name: Rejected Submission Audit Coverage
results.description: Check that all rejected (forged/tampered) submissions appear in the audit log.
results.threat_model: Attacker hopes failed attacks leave no trace.
results.details: 4 SUBMISSION_REJECTED event(s) found in audit log
results.evidence: Audit log captures every rejection with station_code, payload_hash, and reason.
results.recommendation: ✔ Attack evidence is preserved in the audit chain.

results.name: Signature Verification Timing Oracle
results.description: Measure response time: valid sig (37.9 ms) vs random sig (32.9 ms) over 6 rounds each.
results.threat_model: Timing side-channel allows attacker to distinguish near-valid signatures from garbage.
results.details: valid=37.9ms | invalid=32.9ms | diff=5.1ms | Ratio 1.15× — timing difference negligible
results.evidence: Ed25519 uses constant-time verification — cryptography library mitigates timing attacks.

results.name: Paillier Private Key Exposure
results.description: Check whether GET /api/elections/{id} returns the Paillier private key.
results.threat_model: Any client reading the election details can obtain the private key and decrypt any aggregate.
results.evidence: ElectionOut schema includes only paillier_public_key — private key deliberately excluded.
results.recommendation: ✔ Private key is not exposed via the public API.

results.name: Officer Private Key Exposure (Station List)
results.description: Check GET /api/polling/stations for officer_private_key in responses.
results.threat_model: Attacker enumerates stations and steals private keys to forge future submissions.
results.recommendation: ✔ Officer private keys are not exposed in station listings.

results.name: Rejected Submission Forensic Retention
results.description: Verify that forged/tampered submissions are stored with rejection metadata.
results.threat_model: Attacker's failed attack leaves no evidence if rejected submissions are deleted.
results.evidence: rejected=True flag persisted; rejection_reason and payload_hash retained for forensics.
results.recommendation: ✔ Attack evidence is persisted with full forensic detail.

timing_sig.details: valid=37.9ms | invalid=32.9ms | diff=5.1ms | Ratio 1.15× — timing difference negligible
